summary: Large assembly layout-only split that extracted the game-mode title and single-button assembly ranges into asm/melee/gm/gmtitle.s and asm/melee/gm/gmsinglebutton.s, then adjusted obj_files.mk and residual text_N assembly blobs to preserve link order. There was no PR body or review discussion in the slice, so intent is inferred from the title, file moves, and build-object ordering.
what_changed: Added asm/melee/gm/gmtitle.s as a new assembly file with macros.inc and a .text section ending at address 0x801A2220 in the excerpt.
Added asm/melee/gm/gmsinglebutton.s as a new assembly file with macros.inc and a .text section annotated as 0x801A2224 - 0x801A367C.
Removed large contiguous assembly ranges from generic text_4.s/text_5.s blobs and rebalanced remaining code across text_5.s and a newly added text_6.s.
Updated obj_files.mk so the object order became text_4.o, gm/gmtitle.o, gm/gmsinglebutton.o, text_5.o, stage gr objects, code_80224A54.o, then text_6.o.
The diff is dominated by movement/splitting of raw assembly rather than decompiled C or semantic edits.
smart_moves: Placed the extracted files under asm/melee/gm/, matching the game-mode subsystem implied by the filenames rather than leaving them in anonymous text_N blobs.
Inserted gmtitle.o and gmsinglebutton.o directly after text_4.o in obj_files.mk, consistent with their address range around 0x801A0000 and before later text/stage objects.
Created text_6.s to keep the previous large text_5.s content available as a separate object after new split points, reducing risk of link-order drift.
Kept standard assembly-file boilerplate such as .include "macros.inc", .section .text, .global labels, and address comments, making later decompilation and symbol tracking easier.
decomp_lessons: When splitting anonymous asm blobs, preserve exact object order in obj_files.mk; the split files must appear at the same point in the link as their original address range.
Subsystem directories can be introduced before C decompilation: moving gmtitle/gmsinglebutton into asm/melee/gm makes future function naming and C-file replacement more discoverable.
Large text_N reshuffles may look like huge line churn while still being primarily mechanical. Future reviewers should verify object ordering and address continuity rather than assume semantic edits.
Annotating extracted assembly files with their .text address ranges helps future agents confirm that no code was skipped or reordered during extraction.
naming_conventions: Game-mode assembly files used the gm/ directory and lowercase module-style names: gmtitle.s and gmsinglebutton.s.
Build objects mirrored source paths: $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/gm/gmtitle.o and $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/melee/gm/gmsinglebutton.o.
Unidentified symbols retained project-standard placeholder names such as func_801A2224, lbl_80479BC8, jtbl_803DA660, and lbl_804D4248.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Mechanical split of contiguous address ranges from monolithic text_N assembly into subsystem-specific asm files.
Preserved raw instruction comments with virtual/physical addresses, e.g. /* 801A2224 0019EE04 ... */, to maintain traceability.
Preserved jump-table and small-data references in assembly form, including jtbl_803DA660, _SDA_BASE_, and _SDA2_BASE_ references.
Moved object-list entries instead of changing assembly code semantics, which is the safer tactic for early decomp repo organization.
Used a new text_6.s as a continuation bucket for previous generic assembly rather than forcing unrelated later code into the newly named gm files.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
No explicit reviewer rationale or requested changes were available; lessons are inferred from the diff structure.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile asm/melee/gm/gmtitle.s or replace it with C.
Find later PRs that decompile asm/melee/gm/gmsinglebutton.s or rename func_801A2224 and related symbols.
Search for object-order changes around text_4.o, gm/gmtitle.o, gm/gmsinglebutton.o, text_5.o, and text_6.o.
Search for uses or renames of lbl_80479BC8 and jtbl_803DA660 in later gm decompilation work.
